I finally got a closer look at this interesting duck that's been hanging out in Lake Michigan near the Loyola campus for the past couple of weeks. It's a surf scoter.

Darius works in maintenance. this gull injured its leg & wing this week, grounding it at Westerly for the last few days. Yesterday, Darius secured and transported it to the Lake Erie Nature & Science Center for proper care after his shift. Darius. Certified good dude. and gull names are welcome.

By the way, if anybody's intrigued about pigeons and wants to adopt one as a pet, there are many birds that need homes. Try your local SPCA or Palomacy (www.pigeonrescue.org), Great Lakes Pigeon Rescue (greatlakespigeonrescue.org), or another pigeon rescue near you.

33 pound giant snapping turtle was nearly unresponsive when someone found him flailing in Lake Michigan (not a normal habitat). Mystery how he got there. Rescued, and now recovering, but may have pneumonia. www.mlive.com/environment/...
30 seconds of a quiet Lake Erie this evening after sunset, with an evening chorus of Gray Catbirds, Song Sparrows, Yellow Warblers, and more singing in the background.

This one goes out to my friends at for collecting data on the Great Lakes. If you live in the Great Lakes basin you should support them for safer boating, beaching, fishing, paddling, freight hauling and of course…science about me 🌊🌊 seagull.glos.org/support-seag...
I don't often take pictures of the many, many, MANY Ring-billed Gulls at the beach, but yesterday the younger gulls were having too much fun to resist. 😊🧵 #birds
A band of Canadian Cobra Chickens stand guard ready to protect our Border as a mysterious vessel in the distance slowly moves through Canadian waters! 😁
#birds Downy Woodpecker with his Lake Michigan breakfast.
A lone ring-billed gull being buffeted by the winds of November at the beach today. 🪶

Lake Erie is our LIFE SOURCE—we survive and thrive because of its freshwater. It doesn’t belong to any of us—is held in trust between the US and Canada for all. When I was first elected there were only 2 nesting Bald Eagles left on Lake Erie—now thanks to our conservation efforts there are HUNDREDS.

Two Willets on the beach of the south shore of Lake Superior during this spring's migration. Willets are a large stocky shorebirds with #LongLegs. 🪶
#BirdOfTheDay

Coastal wetlands of #GreatLakes come in all shapes and sizes, but the one thing they share in common is how important they are! They're kind of like the rainforests of the #GreatLakes - lots of biodiversity and productivity, and also facing threats from development #WorldWetlandsDay

A favorite photo taken this week of a migrating Lesser Yellowlegs on the shores of Lake Superior in Wisconsin. 🪶
#Stunday #birds #migration

The origins of the name #Prothonotary can spark debate among #birders. These striking, bright-yellow birds — often also called #SwampWarblers — are frequently spotted weaving through reeds in wetland habitats. I photographed these at lake #Erie #metropark in Michigan #birds
#photography
#wildlife

Today’s #BirdOfTheDay theme is #Migratory. Last year we did a long road trip around the country and found ourselves at Magee Marsh, by Lake Erie, as the Warbler migration was starting and saw this Yellow Warbler and more. #birds 🪶 #Nature #wildlife #BirdPhotography #photography #EastCoastKin

A #pair of Northern Shovelers for the #BirdOfTheDay theme that I recently photographed in a marshy area of a city park just south of Lake Superior in Wisconsin. They may breed here, but are considered uncommon residents in Wisconsin.🪶

The light this morning at Lake Erie is magnificent. There is just enough ice for birds to stand in the water and cast a mean reflection. Here is a young Herring Gull taking advantage of the frozen conditions. #birds #birdphotography #omsystem #gulls

As I was driving into town, a Great Blue Heron flew from the direction of Lake Superior over my car disappeared over the bank of Fish Creek. I quickly exited at the boat ramp that I was approaching and scouted around for the bird. Found it!
#BirdsofBluesky

Trumpeter swans are showing up in the Chequamegon Bay of Lake Superior after wintering further south. This one is displaying one of their courtship behaviors. They do nest in the bay area.🪶
#WildlifeWednesday #migration #birds

If you're in the Great Lakes area (Canada or US) and you've got some volunteer time to identify and count #birds and/or #amphibians, the Marsh Monitoring Program needs you! Sign up in Feb for monitoring in the spring www.birdscanada.org/gl_mmp

Anise Hyssop (Agastache foeniculum) is on the endangered list here in Iowa, and considered rare in Illinois. Native to the Midwest, Great Lakes, and Rocky mtn regions, it’s a 3’ tall clumping perennial that doesn’t spread aggressively and is a magnet for bees & butterflies 🐝🦋🌱
Suitable for zones 2-8

Most unicorns are lithe and unostentatious to suit their secretive, forest-dwelling lives - but in harsher climates, you sometimes see more varied shapes and coat patterns. The unicorns of the Great Lakes, for example, have a evolved a certain odd and rugged style all their own. #unicorns #junicorn

#Pileated-Woodpecker (Dryocopus pileatus) & Chicks.... Dryocopus pileatus: Red-capped black and white woodpecker native to North America. Inhabits deciduous forests in eastern North America, the Great Lakes, parts of the Pacific Coast, and the boreal forests of Canada.

Bald Eagle off the shore of the Great Lakes — a testament to Lake Erie conservationists, these great birds are once again flourishing in their natural habitat. 📸Nikon D800 #BirdOfTheDay

A welcome distraction today with this lovely female Vermillion Flycatcher that showed up here! She’s a 1st record for our county & only the 2nd one of this species I’ve seen, the previous being a fly-by male by in TX.
(Video has no sound.) #birds #flycatchers #LakeErie #PresqueIsle #PatchBirding

Trumpeter swans making an appearance here on the big water. I've seen them in the marsh a few times. This is the first time I've seen them out here on Northwest Lake Michigan. #906Life #PureMichigan 🐦‍⬛🪶

I took this photo yesterday from our local Lake Superior oredock platform. Just in time for Alan's #BirdOfTheDay alt theme of #CormorantOrHeron here is the doubled-crested cormorant commonly seen in NW Wisconsin during their breeding season. 🪶
Bald Eagle hunting at sunset a few nights ago. From locally extirpated to expected here, in my lifetime. #birds #sunsets #eagles #LakeErie #conservation

There are about 532,938 acres of #GreatLakes coastal wetlands - some 833 square miles - that provide habitat for nearly 80 species of fish species at some point during their life cycle. Yellow perch, pike, and bass all often start out their lives in these nurseries! #WorldWetlandsDay
A bald eagle just visited here on the beach on Northwest Lake Michigan. #906Life #puremichigan
A brief trip finds me on the north shore of Lake Erie. I met a local fisherman! #birds 🪶

A double-crested cormorant roosting on a piling in Lake Superior in September. 🪶
#BlueSkyMonday #birds

My selection for Alan's #BirdOfTheDay theme of #ScrollBack are my recent photos of a newly arrive male Baltimore Oriole in my yard, an American Eagle in a tree where it had a view of Lake Superior and another recent arrival, the Ruby-throated Hummingbird. 🪶
#birds #Wisconsin #photography
Another giant flock of double-crested cormorants flew north over Lake Michigan near Montrose Point this morning.

The gulls are back! 🪶
#twosday #birds #LakeSuperior

“We abuse land because we see it as a commodity belonging to us. When we see land as a community to which we belong, we may begin to use it with love and respect.” Aldo Leopold Photos from the local park on shore. A place I love, and which teaches me much about biodiversity.

For today’s #BirdOfTheDay theme of #gulls - two months ago I posted another photo of this day back in winter of ‘24, but as you can imagine I have about, oh, three dozen others I love equally. It was one of my favorite lucky captures. Ringed-beak gulls on the snowy shore of Lake Erie. #EastCoastKin

HI IM HERE TO REP THE RESCUE, I got my Flapjack from it and she was mailed as well! Arrived safe and sound, has been a beloved friend ever since :)<3 Please do check out the Great Lakes Pigeon Rescue if you are considering getting a pigeon! 🤍

Double crested cormorants in the Bayview area of Lake Erie. I took this a month ago - no doubt if I were there today, I would see far fewer! On this day, they were as far as the eye could see. Such deft swimmers in the water. #birds #birdwatcher #bird-photography #nature

Who hasn't gone down the rabbit hole after what kind of frogs are heard in Louisiana at night, only to run into a 19th century Black man who owned a circus that toured Great Lakes lumber camps? This is such damn fun! (The frogs soon took a back seat) 😊
Killdeer, acting like a true shorebird at the edge of Lake Superior. #birds 🌿

Views from the Bayfield, Wisconsin Pier on Lake Superior today. Common Loons are swimming toward the breakwater at the beginning and Herring Gulls are seen next. (Phone video since I forgot to put my card back in the camera). 🤪
The first Baltimore Oriole of spring has arrived on the northwest corner of Lake Michigan. #906Life #PureMichigan #birds 🪶

Feeling clever today by combining Friday #scape with the #BirdOfTheDay for today - .bsky.social has challenged us to show photos with a bird or birds against a blue sky. Photo taken December 2024 on the shores of Lake Erie. #birds #EastCoastKin
The gulls are back and frolicking here on the northwest corner of Lake Michigan. #906Life #PureMichigan
#BirdoftheDay #Mr&Mrs along the shore of Lake Erie with their little ones.💕

Caribou used to be everywhere on the shoreline of Lake Superior, but their habitat was gradually whittled away until a pack of wolves was all it took to nearly wipe them out. What can they teach us about bringing a threatened species back from the brink?

Back to Magee Marsh in Ohio on a May morning a few years ago. Prothonotary Warblers (Protonotaria citrea) breed in the woods along Lake Erie, so you can see them tending to their young in nest cavities along the Magee Marsh boardwalk. #warblers #MageeMarsh #birds #birding

Today my Bird of the Day is the Red-winged Blackbird. This one looking from a construction fence out at the vast open waters of Lake Superior as many of us in the US anxiously wait for the one piece of news we most want to hear. 🪶 #itsabird

Happy Thursday friends... yesterday several flocks of Double-crested Cormorants numbering over 21K were counted flying over the western Basin of Lake Erie. Color me WOWWED. In a quieter time, here's a pair chilling on pier pylons. #birds #birdingOH #wildlife #nature #photography #eastcoastkin 🪶🦉📷

Up until that day, I don't think I'd seen more than maybe a dozen red-breasted mergansers at one time. I'm not sure how many this is. A gazillion?
Lake Erie at Cleveland. Jan 9, 2025

Please enjoy these “sure to win awards” photos of waterfowl out on Lake Erie today. Yes, my skills are unmatched. 😂

Happy Labor Day-Monday friends... Spent most of this holiday weekend birding and am going out shortly to finish it off right. Found a Baird's Sandpiper yesterday, working over the shore of Lake Erie among other shorebirds. #fallmigration #birds #birdingOH #wildlife #nature #Photography #EastCoastKIn
Shifting to #birds for the night! Yes, I have 500 pictures of this tern flying around the beach with his fish, trying to get a girl by offering it up, unsuccessfully. Valiant effort, dude.
Today’s highlight from this afternoon’s boat trip out on Lake Erie. Miles and miles from land, just floating around like it’s nothing. #birds #shorebirds #migration #LakeErie
A stunningly photogenic peregrine #falcon for your #Stunday viewing this week. Taken near Palisade Head on the north shore of #LakeSuperior. #photography #bird #wildlife #Minnesota #nikon

I live in a house on a rolling cliff overlooking Lake Superior in Duluth, Minnesota. It is about 2 miles from Hawks Ridge, a place the migrating raptors from central North America travel through in September. #BirdingScotland #UKbirding #orkneybirding

What a special stop on the Birds of the Great Lakes book tour! 💚 Huge thanks to Sydne at Ink Cap Books for hosting such a memorable event. 🪶 And bonus? Sydne told me it was the longest book signing line they’ve ever had at the shop! 🙌🏾 Thank you to everyone who came out to support. Let’s GO! 📖✍🏾🫶🏾

Birds of prey do not like to fly over large bodies of water. So during this time of year their migration south pushes them to fly over the point of Lake Superior. There is a ridge near my house where thousands of birders collect to see this migration

Happy midweek friends... at the Ottawa National Wildlife Refuge, one of the pools contains over 1000 Lesser Scaup scattered far from humans. Then there's this fellow, who hung out with migrating shorebirds all day, begging me to take his photo. #birds #ohbirding #wildlife #nature #LucasCounty
Horned grebes in Lake Michigan near Northerly Island this morning

Barnegat part 11B: Harlequin Ducks edition continued. At Barnegat, these ducks like to hang out on the inlet side of the jetty, fairly close to the jetty walls. When they’ve shown up locally, they tend to hang out by our breakwalls along the beach, where Lake Erie tends to be the roughest. 🪶

Happy Thursday friends... Came across a few mating rituals the other day out by Lake Erie--so fun to watch these Tundra Swans make a lifetime commitment to one another. #birds #birdingOH #wildlife #nature #photography #eastcoastkin #Birdoftheday🪶📷🦉
They pulled this massive snapping turtle out of Lake Michigan near Grand Haven. They think flood waters may have swept it out of a marsh. He is recuperating in Kalamazoo.
Lots of birds on the move in Southeast Michigan by Lake Erie this morning. I saw one huge cloud of something that seemed to be starling or red-winged blackbird sized.

To the best of our* knowledge, these are the first Fish Crows to breed in Columbus Ohio, as the species expands south from Lake Erie. And they chose to nest across the road from my house! So grateful. *the OSU breeding bird team I'm on, plus local eBird reviewers

Summer scene. Seagulls searched the shallow water for fish on a warm June day last year as storms and broken clouds rolled across Lake Michigan into Ludington, MI at sunset. #sunset #water #clouds #birds #beach #lakemichigan #michigan #summer

I've been getting a lot of birds that normally hang out in the marsh out here on the open lake this summer and here is a great blue heron hanging out in NW Lake Michigan this morning. #906Life #PureMichigan 🪶🐦‍⬛ #birds

Bird in the bush. My first attempt at birding armed only with an iPhone. Yellow Warbler, perhaps? I am certain experts can chime in. Photo was taken at Magee Marsh Wildlife Area, along Lake Erie in Ohio. #birdweek #birds #nature #hiking #spring

Pretty sure this was the photo, in case you are wondering (it's blessedly no longer a top google search result). In my defense, it is very cold gulling (looking for gulls) on Lake Erie in January, and also I was thirteen years old.
A couple Black-bellied Plovers that stopped by the beach today, enjoying the lack of people. #birds #shorebirds #LakeErie

Laughing Gulls (Charadriiformes: Laridae: Leucophaeus atricilla) feed on a wide variety of invertebrates and fish, so they're mainly found near coasts. They spend summers as far north as the Great Lakes and Maine and migrate south in winter. Padre Island National Seashore, Texas, 17 Jun 2017.

The alternate #BirdOfTheDay theme today is backlit, which gives me a chance to share a pic from the first time I ever saw American Avocets. Woke up at 6am, saw a post about them in the birders discord, out the door ~7 seconds later in the shirt I slept in, sprinting to the beach. :) Worth it! #birds

It was decades since the bald eagles had nested on Canadian shores of Lake Ontario. By the late 1980’s, mostly as a result of widespread use of the pesticide DDT, there were only 4 active nests in all of the Great Lakes. They were all but locally extinct. #Raptors #Birds shonnahawkins.smugmug.com
Red bellied woodpecker got herself an acorn! On Lake Michigan.
A Nashville Warbler foraging after flying across Lake Erie to Point Pelee. #birds #bird #photography #birdphotography #birder #birding #CanadianBirding #addBirder

Happy Sunday friends... Been running out by Lake Erie recently trying to catch the many duck species in active #Migration, like this Horned Grebe, who thankfully remained in a shipping channel, offering many close-ups. #birds #birdingOH #wildlife #nature #photography #eastcoastkin #BirdoftheDay

Coastal wetlands are some of the most biologically diverse places in the #GreatLakes region, home to numerous fish, invertebrate, reptile, and amphibian species - and that's just in the water! These wetlands are also habitat for birds + mammals, but those are obviously less cool #WorldWildlifeDay
If you conceive a child in Lake Erie, that kids coming out a Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtle.

🪶 The Yellow Warblers of Happiness are coming! As of last weekend they've reached southern OR, WY, the southern Great Lakes and Connecticut.

Another incredible stop on the Birds of the Great Lakes book tour! 📚 Huge thanks to everyone who came out to the Appleton Public Library last night. The local bookseller sold out—let’s gooo! 🙌🏾💚 Grateful for all the love and support, Appleton. #WiscoBirder 🪶

We sure don't get too many great egrets in these parts here on NW Lake Michigan too often but I just was watching one here on my beach doing a little bit of fishing and having some luck. #906Life #mymichigan #birds 🪿

Today I saw my very first bald eagle! It flew across as I was driving into town to go to church. I saw it land in the trees right by some bright blue Lake Michigan waves. It was like driving through a painting. ❤️

I had a wonderful weekend of birding around the marshes of northern Ohio, around Lake Erie. I have many, many pics to sort thru, and I’ll post them as I’m able. In the meantime, here’s a Prothonotary warbler being social and sweet and unfazed by my proximity. He sings at the end! #birds #MageeMarsh

Most handsome duck at the Lake Erie Gun Club. Ruddy duck.
April 23, 2022
This my house. We are up on a cliff overlooking Lake Superior about a mile from Hawks Ridge. I wonder if there are any birders interested in a home exchange? Or people from Kyoto?
Wood Duck among the pads. A male Wood Duck makes its way through lily pads at the Sheldon Marsh State Nature Preserve a warm and sunny Autumn afternoon. #nature #ducks #birds #water #ohio #lakeerie

An Ethic is a Root. In this #woodcut installation, I place an iris over a map of my migration. The iris grows near my childhood home in rural Kansas where agriculture and suburban expansion have made the soil poor. Irises resist erosion and remove soil toxins. Water floods. Soil runs. Roots hold.

One of my favorite bird work memories was the day we took a break from banding birds on the Lake Erie Bass Islands and instead boated over to Kelley's Island and went door-to-door banding the hundreds of baby Purple Martins in the folks' home boxes

A hatch and swarm of midges brought all the Ring-billed Gulls in to take advantage of this aerial bounty. No Herring Gulls joined in because they’re too large and lack the agility to hunt these insects without burning more energy than they would gain. #birds #LakeErie #gulls

A little fall wetland. You can hear White-throated Sparrow scold chips, Hermit Thrush with some autumnal recrudescence singing, Dark-eyed Juncos firing their lasers in a skirmish, and a young White-throated Sparrow practicing what he heard in summer.
#birds #fall #LakeErie #NatureSounds

Two years ago in May we had a colorful visitor to the Lake Erie shore. Purple Gallinules are residents of the deep south. This wasn't the first one to show up here, but it was pretty special nonetheless. #birds, #photography

The spring birding on Lake Erie is world-famous for a reason. (Our version of famous, anyway.) On many occasions the warblers are indeed everywhere and close. E.g., this pic I took over someone’s shoulder. 🪶 cc: ‪.bsky.social‬

A great read about the environment and muskrats: With their surgical nibbles, these large, semi-aquatic, buck-toothed rodents have emerged as crucial helpers for ecologists restoring degraded wetlands across the Great Lakes. By .bsky.social www.chicagotribune.com/2025/11/04/m...

Headed to the 2026 Annual Gull Frolic as the featured speaker and I’m fired up to see my gull loving homies. 🐦 Big shoutout to Amar Ayyash and Illinois Audubon for the invite. Second leg of the Birds of the Great Lakes Book Tour is officially underway. You ready? Let’s Go! 🪶🫶🏾

Happy midweek friends... #Determination can be seen as this Black and White Warbler works their way down this vine in search for nutrients--to help fuel their flight across Lake Erie into Canada. #Birdoftheday #birds #birdingOH #wildlife #nature #photography 🦉📷🪶

Today my Bird of the Day is the Common Merganser. This one found late last spring in Two Harbors, MN. It’s hard to imagine a prettier place but swimming in Lake Superior in May is not my idea of a good time. 🪶

A Solitary Sandpiper strutting along a mud flat.
My friend Joe Kwasniewski has a phenomenal presentation on shorebirds around the Great Lakes. These birds can be so tricky to identify! Check out "Shorebird ID 101 with Joe Kwasniewski": www.youtube.com/watch?v=DMQJ...

Not a great photo but an interesting sight. This was the first really large flock of Red-breasted Mergansers on the lakefront that I have seen this fall. Several hundred in a stretched out flock about half a mile out into the lake being harassed by thieving gulls.

This tiny red squirrel that visits everyday for some peanuts is actually taking the time and pondering the view of northwest Lake Michigan. #906Life #PureMichigan

So-let’s enjoy this Heron walking along on a Florida shore.🤣☀️😎
Lake Erie shores were unbelievable (due to the incredibly heavy winds). Please stay safe & warm- w/be back after dinners🤗
Gulls on a drizzly morning. Indiana Dunes, Lake Michigan, May 2024.

#birds

#lakeerie I have been hearing him for a while. He finally came in to pose. Red-shoulder Hawk. A real beauty.
Who wants to help me find Birds of the Great Lakes at Barnes & Noble? You Ready? Let’s GO!!!! 📖👐🏾🫶🏾🪶
Saw a lifer Harlequin Duck today! My photos aren't great, but you can definitely tell what bird it is. Rare to see in this part of Wisconsin (away from Lake Michigan) 🪶 #birds
A black-throated green warbler at the tip of Long Point on Lake Erie. The two hour trip across pounding waves in a zodiac was more then worth it. #birds #birding #nature #photography

Visited Lorain Impoundment Lakeside Landing for the first time today. Looked like an excellent birding spot but there wasn't a lot of variety today. The pier had quite a few people fishing for yellow perch in spite of the big winds and choppy water. Word was nothing was biting ... yet.

Happy new week friends... Northern Pintails have been gathering in the marshes below Lake Erie, waiting for favorable winds to help carry them to their summer nesting grounds. of They are one of the prettiest ducks. #birds #birdingOH #wildlife #nature #photography #eastcoastkin #birdoftheday
I definitely can't resist a waxwing party! From on top of Oberg Mountain, on the North shore of Lake Superior. #birds 🪶
Face off on a log. Two Painted Turtles square off in a battle for the top spot on the log. At the Sheldon Marsh State Wildlife Preserve. #turtles #marsh #wetland #nature #water #lakeerie #ohio

Frogs + toads that inhabit #GreatLakes coastal wetlands are more than just cute - they also can tell us about a wetland's health! With Great Lakes Restoration Initiative Funding, scientists use amphibian monitoring data to understand changing conditions #WorldFrogDay 🐸 www.epa.gov/great-lakes-...

When I was a kid, we could hear Northern Bobwhite's calls in our neighborhood, then one day they all disappeared. That is until last week, when a small flock (covey) showed up singing their name, near the western basin of Lake Erie. Thankfully three posed for me. #Birds #BirdingOH #NorthernBobwhite

Happy Tuesday friends... my birding vacation is going great so far, and here's a beautiful non-breeding, Bonapart's Gull I captured, while walking along the shores of Lake Erie. #birds #birdingOH #wildlife #nature #photography #Birdoftheday #ECK #laridae 🦉📷🪶

Northern Parula warblers checking out an Autumn Olive for things to eat as they migrate through Michigan. They do not breed around the Great Lakes but breed south and north of this area; Usnea lichens in the north for nesting. Industrial air pollutants killed off Usnea lichens around the Great Lakes

White-throated sparrow in a gloomy spot in the forest. They were all over the southern coast of Lake Erie both yesterday and today in the Cleveland area. Didn't see any last weekend in the exact same spots.

Local birders (south shore of Lake Superior), are saying that this is a young Ross's Goose. one of the three white geese that breed in North America.

Yesterday’s stop on the Birds of the Great Lakes Book Tour took me to Elkhart, Indiana — and wow, what an incredible experience! Huge shoutout to Fables Books and the Wellfield Botanical Gardens for hosting me. What an amazing group and such a stunning place to share Bird Joy. 🫶🏾🪶

In Rogers Park, on the far north side, the feral murals never stay on the same wall for very long. When you find them again they’re in front of a shop that closed down years ago, but here it is open; or next to a beach with a piano on it; or looking over a sculpture garden that sprang up overnight.
We were out looking for migratory ducks on Lake Erie. Didn't see much until we crossed the Cedar Point causeway. #photography #📷 #birds #LakeErie #Ohio

#BirdOfTheDay, #BirdsOnTwigs I caught this Bay Breasted Warbler during spring migration at Magee Marsh on Lake Erie in Ohio. This location brings birders from across the world to witness concentrations of warblers as they pass through. Amazing. #birds #birdphotography #birdwatching #birdmigration

Happy Tuesday friends... Over the weekend, I saw a MURDER of American crows, probably 75-100 in total, tree hopping at a small woodlot close to Lake Erie. Not the greatest photo but a witness to their migration. #birds #birdingOH #wildlife #nature #photography #Eastcoastkin #Birdoftheday 📷🪶🦉

Migrating Trumpeter swans in the Chequamegon Bay on the south shore of Lake Superior in Wisconsin.

My wetlands class monitors a coastal peatland transitioning to marsh. Annually, we see hybrid cattail encroach the fen, but the advance of the native Phragmites has been most notable. This year, almost all of the Phragmites died; perhaps a pulse-stable process with recent high water! Typha lives on.
